title: Unsalted SHA-256 for password storage
description: Passwords are stored as plain SHA-256 hashes without per-user salt. The seedDefaultDataIfNeeded function stores 'admin'.sha256() and ''.sha256(). The normalizedPasswordForStorage function at ServerController.swift:2514 either accepts a pre-hashed value or calls .sha256() on plaintext. SHA-256 is fast and unsalted, making it vulnerable to rainbow table attacks and GPU-accelerated brute-force. Two users with the same password will have identical hashes.
recommendation: Migrate to bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2id for password storage. Add per-user random salt. The protocol-level ECDH key exchange protects the password in transit, but the at-rest storage remains weak. Note: this is a protocol-level design decision that requires careful migration planning.
